gaps: A petition-specific prior that separates modern cert petitions from historical published-opinion records would improve calibration for sparse SCOTUS snapshots.
A snapshot field summarizing why an event remains in predict scope would help distinguish genuinely pending petitions from unresolvable historical records.
helpful: fedcourts paths resolved the event and snapshot-adjacent ledger paths.
fedcourts stats provided the SCOTUS petition-disposition base-rate anchor.
Existing schemas and outcome code clarified how `other` and binary granted are scored.
notes: The case snapshot itself carried very little case-specific information, so the prediction relied primarily on local SCOTUS base rates and the snapshot-visible cluster reference.
